day_trips_wine.name: The Saar valley (Saar Riesling)
day_trips_wine.description: The Saar tributary makes Germany's most piercing, mineral Riesling around Wiltingen and Saarburg, cooler and racier than the Middle Mosel and slow to ripen.
day_trips_wine.tip: Base the day around Wiltingen and Saarburg; pair a top estate visit with the floodlit waterfall in Saarburg's town centre.

day_trips_wine.name: The Ruwer valley (Mertesdorf and Kasel)
day_trips_wine.description: A short hop from Trier, the tiny Ruwer makes ageless cool-climate Riesling, with Mertesdorf home to the historic Maximin Gruenhaus monopole and Kasel nearby.
day_trips_wine.tip: The Ruwer is small enough to fold into a Trier afternoon; combine a Mertesdorf estate with the Roman monuments in the city.

day_trips_wine.name: Trier (Roman wine city)
day_trips_wine.description: Germany's oldest city and the region's Roman capital pairs the Porta Nigra with charity wine cellars on Roman foundations and historic Mosel, Saar and Ruwer estates.
day_trips_wine.tip: See the Roman wine-ship relief in the Rheinisches Landesmuseum, then taste in a city Weinstube; Trier links all three river valleys.

day_trips_wine.name: The Nahe (Bad Kreuznach)
day_trips_wine.description: Between the Mosel and Rheinhessen, the Nahe makes Riesling now ranked beside the best Mosel and Rheingau, on varied volcanic and slate soils around Bad Kreuznach.
day_trips_wine.tip: The Nahe's varied soils make for striking side-by-side tasting; it is a longer drive, so make it a full day rather than a half.

day_trips_wine.name: Luxembourg Moselle (Remich and Grevenmacher)
day_trips_wine.description: Across the border the same river becomes Luxembourg's Moselle, focused on traditional-method Cremant and Auxerrois, with riverside towns Remich and Grevenmacher.
day_trips_wine.tip: Visit the rock-cut cellars at Grevenmacher or Remich for Cremant; the contrast with Mosel Riesling makes a rewarding cross-border day.

day_trips_wine.name: The Rheingau (Ruedesheim)
day_trips_wine.description: Down the Rhine from Koblenz, the Rheingau is Germany's other Riesling heartland, fuller than the Mosel, with Ruedesheim and the Kloster Eberbach monastery.
day_trips_wine.tip: A long day or better an overnight; ride the Ruedesheim cable car for vineyard views and taste at Kloster Eberbach for the historical contrast.
